title: Who Would Be Freed by My Visible Wealth?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for exploring the fears that keep financial success hidden, and who specifically would be helped by seeing it openly
excerpt: A journal prompt set from the Day 82 theme of a 12-week money programme. The prompts open with a wide look at the fear of being visible in abundance, then narrow to the specific person whose hope or permission would be given by seeing yours.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 82 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you feel the habit of muting or qualifying financial success around specific people, and want to understand what is keeping you there.

content.text: A journal prompt set from the Day 82 theme of a 12-week money programme. Day 82: inspire others through your wealth. The prompts look at the fears that produce the hiding habit, and at the specific person whose own journey would be helped by seeing what you have built.
New to journal prompts as practice? Read
What fears still come up about being visible in abundance?
Write the specific worry, not the general one.
How does it feel to know that my ease gives others permission to expand?
Write whatever comes, including the discomfort if it is there.
What truth can replace the fear that my success makes others uncomfortable?
Write a single true sentence that holds the reframe.
Who in my life would be most encouraged by seeing my financial confidence openly?
Name the specific person. Write what it might give them.
Who am I grateful for who mirrors my light back to me?
Name them. Write one thing they do.
Adapted from the Day 82 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
